revision.comment: Bot: remove [[Category:Sn crud]] (crud category cleanup) [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/23986660476|commit triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Chalybeate]][[fr:Eau ferrugineuse]][[ja:含鉄泉]][[simple:Chalybeate]]
[[File:Beppu Chinoike-jigoku01n4272.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Example of an iron-containing spring ({{ill|Beppu_Onsen|en|Beppu_Onsen|qid=Q4890932|lt=Shibaseki Onsen|1=Beppu Onsen}} {{ill|Hells_of_Beppu|en|Hells_of_Beppu|qid=Q5709027|lt=Chinoike Jigoku|1=Hells of Beppu}})]] '''Iron-Containing Spring''' (含鉄泉, Gantetsu-sen) is a type of {{ill|Onsen|en|Onsen|qid=Q655311|lt=Onsen|1=Onsen}} water quality classified under the {{ill|Medical Spring|ja|温泉#療養泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Medical Spring|1=Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q655311}} category based on labeling for its special components.
== Overview == There are two types of iron-containing springs: those of the {{ill|Bicarbonate Spring|ja|炭酸水素塩泉|1=Bicarbonate springs|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bicarbonate Spring|13=User:Immanuelle/Bicarbonate springs|qq=}} type and those of the {{ill|Sulfate spring|ja|硫酸塩泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sulfate spring|qq=}} type. A distinguishing characteristic of iron-containing springs is their tendency to exhibit {{ill|Yellow|en|Yellow|qid=Q943|lt=Yellow|1=Yellow}}, {{ill|Red|ja|赤色|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Red|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 赤|1=Red|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q3142}} , or {{ill|Reddish Brown|ja|赤褐色|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reddish Brown|1=Reddish Brown|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11635977}} hues due to oxidation after emergence. Most {{ill|Red Spring|ja|赤湯|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Red Spring|qq=}} hot springs are iron-rich springs. Additionally, some iron-containing springs may also include {{ill|Carbonic acid|en|Carbonic acid|qid=Q104334|lt=Carbonation|1=Carbonic acid}}, making them {{ill|Carbonated Springs|ja|炭酸泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Carbonated Springs|qq=}}
== Definition == An iron-containing spring is defined as a spring that contains a total iron ion (Fe<sup>2+</sup>, Fe<sup>3+</sup>) content of at least 20 mg per kilogram of water.
== Comparison of Old and New Labels == Below is a comparison of the old and new water quality labels for iron-containing springs:
{| class="wikitable" |+ Comparison of Old and New Onsen Water Quality Labels<ref>{{Cite web|title=Reference Material: Comparison of Old and New Onsen Water Quality Labels |url=https://www.env.go.jp/nature/onsen/docs/senshitsumei26.pdf |format=PDF |publisher=Natural Environment Bureau, Ministry of the Environment |accessdate=2018-03-26 }} </ref> ! Old Label !! New Label !! Abbreviated Label |- style="background-color:#eef" | Iron Spring || Iron Spring || Fe-Spring |- | Carbonate Iron Spring || (Iron(II)-Bicarbonate Spring) || (Fe(II)-HCO<sub>3</sub>-type) |- | Simple Carbonate Iron Spring || Simple Iron(II) Spring (Bicarbonate Type) || Simple Fe(II)-Spring (HCO<sub>3</sub>-type) |- | Carbonated Iron Spring || Carbonated & Iron-Containing Calcium Bicarbonate Spring || Cont. Fe(II)･CO<sub>2</sub>-Ca-HCO<sub>3</sub>-Spring |- | Alum & Green Vitriol Spring | {{Plainlist| * Aluminum & Iron(II)-Sulfate Spring * Or Iron(II)-Aluminum-Sulfate Spring }}
| {{Plainlist| * Al･Fe(II)-SO<sub>4</sub>-Spring * Cont. Fe(II)-Al-SO<sub>4</sub>-Spring }}
|}
== Health Benefits == ''Note: [[Health effects of hot springs|Effects]] are not guaranteed for all individuals.''
The following benefits are associated with iron-containing springs: * '''Bathing''': In addition to {{ill|General Indications|ja|一般的適応症|1=General Indications for hot spring use|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/General Indications|13=User:Immanuelle/General Indications for hot spring use|qq=}} , they are said to help with {{ill|Menstrual disorders|en|Menstrual disorder|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Menstrual disorders|qq=}}
* '''Drinking''': Claimed to be effective for treating {{ill|Anemia|en|Anemia|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anemia|qq=}}
== Representative Onsen Locations == * [[Ikaho Onsen]] (Gunma Prefecture) * {{ill|Arima Onsen|en|Arima Onsen|qid=Q4790673|lt=Arima Onsen|1=Arima Onsen}} (Hyōgo Prefecture)
== Notes == <references />
== Related Articles == * [[Red Spring]]
